content.problemStatement:
Given A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC as the sides of a triangle, find whether the triangle is scalene.

Note:


A triangle is said to be scalene if all three sides of the triangle are **distinct**.
It is guaranteed that the sides represent a valid triangle.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three space-separated integers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC — the length of the three sides of the triangle.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES`, if the triangle is scalene, and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase. For example, `YES`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `yEs` are all considered identical.
constraints: A, B, C satisfy the triangle inequality (A + B > C, A + C > B, B + C > A).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C — the lengths of the sides of a triangle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the triangle is scalene (all three sides are pairwise different) or "NO" otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: sides 2,3,4 are all different → YES.
Test case 2: sides 1,2,2 have two equal sides → NO.
Test case 3: sides 2,2,2 are all equal → NO.
Test case 4: sides 3,5,6 are all different → YES.
First triangle has two equal sides (5,5,6) → NOT scalene. Second triangle 7,8,9 has all distinct sides → scalene.
All three sides are equal, so the triangle is not scalene.

solution.approach: For each test case read the three side lengths. A triangle is scalene iff the three values are all distinct, i.e., A != B, B != C and A != C. Because the input is guaranteed to form a valid triangle, no further checks are needed. Output "YES" when the condition holds, otherwise "NO".
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
